summary: 2x2 {2ch,5ch}x{log,nolog} under planar. Effects ADDITIVE and act on DIFFERENT metrics: interaural cues -> d1 (+0.015, angle); log1p -> rmse (-0.010, range). Predicted interaction (log helps more at 2ch) REFUTED.
RayDPT re-anchored under planar. 5 epochs only (713s/ep), best=last: compute-starved, not beaten. Not a verdict.
Temporal-resolution arms both improved composite but through OPPOSITE metrics: density arm bought RMSE, resolution arm bought d1. Predicted range mechanism unsupported. E6 confounded 3 ways.
Converged RayDPT (21ep, best ep18) beats starved E4 by 0.1162 = 14 sigma. D5 confirmed. Needed a capacity cut (decode32, 1 cross layer), licensed by I2's oracle.
Restoring 2 cross layers did not converge (15ep, best=last), so its d1 is a lower bound. Question unanswered. Revealed the real constraint: the COST of a cross layer, not their number.

First converged 2-layer RayDPT. 2x2 attribution: coarse KV +0.0033 d1, 2nd layer +0.0046, additive, neither clears sigma. Full-val spatial diagnostic then OVERTURNED D9: the deficit is far-field range compression, not angles.

Both relative dense terms made the 7-10m deciles WORSE. Dense MAE is NOT the cause of far-field compression. rel_mae's 1/gt weighting moved the error budget to NEAR pixels (my sign error, reproduced in data); log_mae is symmetric and still hurts, so relativity itself is wrong here.
